events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "medium",
  "work_stage": "in-service",
  "component_conditions": [
    "degraded"
  ],
  "percent_complete": 100,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Plastic shrinkage cracking observed on the concrete surface",
    "Rapid evaporation of surface moisture exceeded the rate of bleeding during finishing/curing"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Perform a structural and durability assessment of the cracks",
    "Seal or inject cracks with low-viscosity epoxy or polyurethane if depth and width compromise reinforcement protection",
    "Review and adjust curing procedures for future pours to include windbreaks, fogging, or immediate curing compound application"
  ],
  "rationale": "Plastic shrinkage cracks occur when the rate of surface evaporation exceeds the rate at which bleed water rises to the concrete surface during the finishing and early curing stages. This rapid drying causes tensile stresses in the plastic concrete that lead to cracking. Because the asset is in-service and the cracks can compromise durability and allow ingress of corrosive agents, the work is graded as a fail with a degraded condition.",
  "references": [
    "ACI 302.1R (Guide for Concrete Floor and Slab Construction)",
    "ACI 308R (Guide to External Curing of Concrete)"
  ]
}
